clauses.explanation: Interest rate is floating and may be revised quarterly based on the lender's internal benchmark. You will be notified by SMS or email 7 days prior.
clauses.reason: Rate can change but notice is given. The 7-day window is short but better than no notice at all.

clauses.explanation: No prepayment is allowed for the first 12 months. After that, a prepayment charge of 4% applies on the outstanding principal.
clauses.reason: 12-month lock-in and 4% prepayment fee are above market average. Limits your ability to refinance if you find a better rate.

clauses.explanation: Penal interest of 3% per month applies on overdue amounts from the date of default, compounded monthly.
clauses.reason: 3% per month is 36% annualised — significantly above standard. Monthly compounding makes this escalate very fast.

clauses.explanation: A default is triggered if any EMI is overdue by more than 3 days. No prior notice is required before the default is recorded.
clauses.reason: 3-day trigger with no grace period is aggressive. A single delayed NACH debit can put you in default.

clauses.explanation: You consent to sharing your financial and personal data with group companies, credit bureaus, and third-party analytics partners.
clauses.reason: Broad but standard for NBFCs. Worth knowing your data will be shared beyond the lender.

clauses.explanation: In case of default, the lender will issue a 15-day notice before initiating recovery. You may cure the default within this period.
clauses.reason: 15-day cure window is short but present. Better than no-notice enforcement.

major_risks: Prepayment locked for 12 months — you cannot refinance even if rates drop
Penal interest at 36% annualised compounds monthly — debt can spiral fast
Default triggered after just 3 days overdue with no prior warning
